industries.name: Medical Lasers & Imaging
uses: Tm:YAG and Tm:fiber lasers at 2013nm: an emerging surgical laser wavelength combining the advantages of Ho:YAG (2100nm) and Er:YAG (2940nm) — superior tissue ablation efficiency with lower peak power requirements. Used in urology for transurethral resection of the prostate (ThuLEP) and kidney stone ablation

Thulium fiber laser (TFL) at 1940nm for holmium stone ablation fragmentation: TFL achieves superior stone dusting compared to Ho:YAG with a more flexible fiber — rapidly displacing holmium as the standard for urological laser lithotripsy

Thulium-170 (¹⁷⁰Tm, t½ = 128.6 days, beta emitter) as radiation source in portable X-ray machines for field radiography — requires no electrical power, useful for medical imaging in remote locations and in developing-world field hospitals
Tm³⁺-doped fiber amplifiers for 1900–2100nm wavelength range — expanding fiber optic amplification to the short-wave infrared (SWIR) band for next-generation optical communications and sensing

companies.name: IPG Photonics
companies.use: World's largest fiber laser manufacturer. Produces thulium-doped fiber lasers at 1940nm and 2013nm for medical, materials processing, and scientific applications. Its TLR-series Tm-fiber lasers are used in urology and soft tissue surgery.

companies.name: Quanta System
companies.use: Major medical laser company producing Tm:YAG and Ho:YAG laser systems for urological procedures including kidney stone lithotripsy and prostate surgery. Produces the CYBER TM thulium laser system for urological applications.

industries.name: Specialty Applications
uses: Thulium oxide (Tm₂O₃) as an infrared transmitting material — thulium compounds transmit in the 2–5 μm mid-infrared region used in chemical sensing and environmental monitoring

Thulium-doped upconverting nanoparticles (UCNPs): Tm³⁺ produces blue and UV upconversion emission under 980nm NIR excitation — used in bioimaging, multiphoton microscopy, and anti-counterfeiting inks visible only under NIR illumination
Thulium in specialty alloys: added to nickel superalloys in small amounts to improve high-temperature oxidation resistance in gas turbine applications
Thulium is one of the least abundant and most expensive naturally occurring lanthanides — global production is only a few tonnes per year, limiting commercial applications to high-value niches

companies.name: Luminova / NanoHorizons
companies.use: Produces thulium-containing upconverting nanoparticles for bioimaging, diagnostic assay, and anti-counterfeiting applications using Tm³⁺/Yb³⁺ co-doped systems that emit blue and UV light under near-infrared excitation.
